rules.rule: Serve every page over HTTPS with a valid cert; HSTS where possible.
rules.why: Non-HTTPS is a ranking penalty and AEO trust signal.

rules.rule: Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1 on mobile.
rules.why: Google page-experience signal.

rules.rule: One <h1> per page, matching the primary query intent.
rules.why: Multiple H1s dilute topical clarity for both Google and LLM extractors.

rules.rule: Unique <title> (50-60 chars) and <meta name="description"> (140-160 chars) per URL.
rules.why: Duplicates trigger demotion; short/missing meta loses SERP CTR.

rules.rule: Canonical tag on every indexable page; self-referential unless intentionally consolidating.
rules.why: Prevents duplicate-content dilution.

rules.rule: XML sitemap linked from robots.txt; updated within 24h of publish.
rules.why: Speeds discovery and indexation.

rules.rule: Internal links use descriptive anchor text, not 'click here' or raw URLs.
rules.why: Anchor text is a ranking signal and an AEO context signal.

rules.rule: Image alt text on every non-decorative image; decorative images alt="".
rules.why: Accessibility + image search + AEO content understanding.

rules.rule: Mobile-first layout: tap targets >= 44x44px, no horizontal scroll at 360px width.
rules.why: Google ranks mobile-first.

rules.rule: Intrusive interstitials on mobile (cookie walls exempt if dismissible in one tap).
rules.why: Interstitial penalty.

rules.rule: Duplicate content across URLs without a canonical consolidating them.
rules.why: Signal dilution; potential demotion.

rules.rule: Render-blocking JS that pushes LCP past 2.5s. Defer or inline critical CSS.
rules.why: Direct Core Web Vitals hit.

rules.rule: Orphan pages (no internal links in). Every indexable page needs >=1 internal link.
rules.why: Orphans get de-prioritized for crawl and rank.

rules.rule: Accidental noindex on production — audit every template's meta robots before publish.
rules.why: Silently kills traffic.

rules.rule: Chains of 301 redirects longer than 1 hop.
rules.why: Link-equity loss and crawl budget waste.

rules.rule: Lead with the answer. First 150 words must directly answer the primary query.
rules.why: Both snippet extraction and LLM citation pull from the top of the page.

rules.rule: Target one primary query per page; supporting queries as H2s.
rules.why: Topic focus wins; splitting intent kills both pages.

rules.rule: Cite primary sources (gov't, academic, original research) with outbound links.
rules.why: E-E-A-T + AEO trust signal.

rules.rule: Author byline with credentials; bio page with sameAs to LinkedIn/ORCID.
rules.why: Entity authority for AEO, EEAT for Google.

rules.rule: Publish date + last-updated date visible on page and in schema.
rules.why: Freshness ranking + LLM citation confidence.

rules.rule: Short paragraphs (<=3 sentences), frequent H2/H3, bulleted lists for scannable facts.
rules.why: Matches both human scan and LLM chunking.

rules.rule: Include an FAQ section covering the top 3 related PAA queries.
rules.why: Captures People Also Ask + feeds FAQPage schema.

rules.rule: AI-generated prose published without a human editor reviewing it.
rules.why: Drafts must go through admin approval per product policy.

rules.rule: Keyword stuffing; target density < 2% on primary query.
rules.why: Spam signal; can trigger manual action.

rules.rule: Thin pages (< 300 words) on commercial intent queries.
rules.why: Google's helpful content system demotes thin pages.

rules.rule: Doorway pages — near-duplicate pages varying only by city/keyword.
rules.why: Policy violation; potential manual action.

rules.rule: Fake or undisclosed AI authorship bylines.
rules.why: Trust collapse when detected; regulatory risk in some jurisdictions.

rules.rule: Answer in a standalone sentence near the top — LLMs extract single sentences.
rules.why: If the answer is spread across paragraphs, the page won't be cited.

rules.rule: Use entity-rich phrasing: 'Stripe Radar (Stripe's fraud product)' not 'their fraud tool'.
rules.why: LLMs drop sentences with ambiguous references.

rules.rule: Facts in tables where possible; LLMs parse tables better than prose for numeric data.
rules.why: Tables are extracted structurally and cited with high confidence.

rules.rule: Include a brief, explicit 'What is X?' definition even when the whole page is about X.
rules.why: That sentence is what gets quoted in answer-engine cards.

rules.rule: Publish /about and /authors/{name} pages; answer engines lean hard on entity authority.
rules.why: Identity pages strengthen citation weight across the whole site.

rules.rule: Use stable, human-readable URLs (/guides/stripe-radar-pricing), not /p?id=4892.
rules.why: Readable URLs are themselves a context signal to LLMs.

rules.rule: Include a TL;DR block above the fold.
rules.why: summary extraction prefers dedicated TL;DR blocks.

rules.rule: Offer a structured facts block ('Founded: 2010 · HQ: SF · Employees: 9,000').
rules.why: This is what gets lifted into Perplexity cards and ChatGPT citations.

rules.rule: Keep the primary answer stable across time; wholesale rewrites reset LLM trust.
rules.why: LLM caches reward stability.

rules.rule: Burying the answer under SEO intro fluff ('In today's fast-paced world…').
rules.why: LLM extractors skip the page.

rules.rule: Pronoun-only references ('it', 'this', 'the company') in fact-bearing sentences.
rules.why: Extracted sentence is ambiguous and gets dropped.

rules.rule: Facts only inside images or video.
rules.why: LLMs can't cite what they can't read.

rules.rule: Paywall / login wall the primary answer.
rules.why: Gated answers don't get cited.

rules.rule: Contradicting prior content without explicit 'Updated YYYY-MM-DD: previously X, now Y.'
rules.why: LLMs notice contradictions and downrank.

rules.rule: Over-anthropomorphized AI-slop phrases ('Let's dive in!', 'Buckle up!', 'In today's fast-paced world', 'In conclusion').
rules.why: Strong AI-slop signals, getting filtered by answer engines.

rules.rule: Use JSON-LD, not Microdata or RDFa. One <script type="application/ld+json"> per type.
rules.why: JSON-LD is Google's preferred format and cleanest to validate.

rules.rule: Match schema content to visible page content exactly.
rules.why: Structured-data spam policy triggers on mismatches.

rules.rule: Every page: WebPage + BreadcrumbList. Add Article/Product/Service as appropriate.
rules.why: Baseline context every extractor relies on.

rules.rule: Organization schema on the homepage with name, url, logo, sameAs (social + Wikipedia + Crunchbase).
rules.why: Anchors entity identity across the whole site.

rules.rule: FAQPage schema when a visible FAQ section exists. Never fake one.
rules.why: Fake FAQs violate policy; real FAQs win rich results.

rules.rule: HowTo schema on step-by-step guides with step arrays.
rules.why: Unlocks how-to rich result.

rules.rule: author as a full Person entity with url, sameAs, jobTitle. Strings are downweighted.
rules.why: EEAT author entity graph.

rules.rule: datePublished + dateModified in ISO 8601.
rules.why: Freshness signal + avoids parser warnings.

rules.rule: Review schema for reviews that aren't visible on the page.
rules.why: Direct policy violation.

rules.rule: Aggregate rating without an actual reviewCount source.
rules.why: Policy violation.

rules.rule: HowTo on marketing pages that aren't actually how-tos.
rules.why: Spam signal.

rules.rule: Stuffing irrelevant types (Event schema on a product page).
rules.why: Dilutes trust, can trigger manual review.

rules.rule: Mixing @type inside a single JSON-LD block incorrectly.
rules.why: Parses fail; rich results disappear.

rules.rule: Separate ads from content visually; never mimic navigation, answers, or download buttons.
rules.why: AdSense policy + user trust.

rules.rule: Label sponsored and affiliate content ('Sponsored', rel="sponsored", rel="nofollow").
rules.why: Required by Google policy and FTC.

rules.rule: Publish Privacy Policy, Terms, and Contact page linked from every footer.
rules.why: Required for AdSense approval and AEO trust.

rules.rule: Cookie consent banner for EU/UK traffic (TCF v2.2 if serving personalized ads).
rules.why: GDPR / UK GDPR compliance.

rules.rule: Max 3 ad units above the fold on desktop, 1 on mobile.
rules.why: Preserves LCP and user trust.

rules.rule: Use loading="lazy" on below-the-fold ad containers.
rules.why: Core Web Vitals protection.

rules.rule: Click-bait ad placement that obscures the primary answer.
rules.why: Policy violation + AEO demotion.

rules.rule: Auto-play audio or unskippable interstitial ads.
rules.why: Policy violation; demoted by both Google and users.

rules.rule: Pop-ups on entry (exit-intent allowed; user-triggered allowed).
rules.why: Interstitial penalty.

rules.rule: Thin content pages served primarily to host ads.
rules.why: AdSense policy violation and instant AEO disqualifier.

rules.rule: Artificial traffic inflation or paid clicks.
rules.why: Instant AdSense ban.

rules.rule: More ad weight than content weight above the fold (>40% ad pixels).
rules.why: Policy violation and UX collapse.
